name: Young mitochondria reduced stress-linked apoptosis markers in aged rat PFC
summary: An aged-rat stress model adds CNS mechanistic support for young mitochondrial transplantation.
statement: In 22-month-old male rats, a single intracerebroventricular administration of fresh mitochondria from young rat brain reduced prefrontal-cortex oxidative-stress and apoptosis markers, including lower MDA, Bax, caspase-3, and cytochrome c release and higher Bcl-2 in the chronic-stress context.
population_or_model: Aged male rats with or without four weeks of chronic stress
time_horizon: Four-week chronic stress model with single mitochondrial administration
quantitative_note: The paper reports n = 3 for plotted prefrontal-cortex biomarker comparisons.
caveats: The evidence is a small animal biomarker study, not a behavioral or human efficacy result.
Intracerebroventricular delivery is invasive and not a practical general aging intervention.
The chronic-stress model is an injury/stress context rather than normal human aging.
